tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hello! I'm working on genealogical research focused on the Nouvelle-Aquitaine region in France, and I'd like to save my research preferences so they're remembered for future sessions. I have specific ways I like to organize my work - things like preferring detailed source documentation and focusing on church records from the 18th and 19th centuries. Could you help me store these preferences?
I'd be happy to help you store your genealogical research preferences! Let me save those for future sessions.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Genealogical research preferences for Nouvelle-Aquitaine region in France: prefers detailed source documentation; focuses on church records from the 18th and 19th centuries."}

messages.content:
Done! I've stored your research preferences with the following details:

- **Region focus:** Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France
- **Documentation style:** Detailed source documentation
- **Record focus:** Church records from the 18th and 19th centuries

These will be retrieved automatically in future sessions. Let me know if you'd like to add or modify anything!

Great, thank you for saving those! Before I dive into a new research query, could you show me what's been stored so I can make sure I have all my previous context handy? I'd like to verify that everything is there before I start looking into some new church records.

{"status": "success", "memories": [{"id": "mem_001", "text": "Genealogical research preferences for Nouvelle-Aquitaine region in France: prefers detailed source documentation; focuses on church records from the 18th and 19th centuries.", "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "count": 1}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what's currently stored:

| ID | Created | Preference |
|----|---------|------------|
| mem_001 | 2024-01-15 | Genealogical research preferences for Nouvelle-Aquitaine region in France: prefers detailed source documentation; focuses on church records from the 18th and 19th centuries. |

Everything looks good - your preferences are saved and ready to go! What would you like to research next?

That's helpful, thank you! I can see my main preferences are stored. But I'd like to make sure I haven't missed anything else - is there only the one memory entry, or are there any other pieces of information stored that I should know about?

Based on the list results, there's only **one memory entry** stored (mem_001) - the one we just reviewed with your Nouvelle-Aquitaine research preferences. There don't appear to be any other pieces of information stored.

You're all set to start your research! Let me know when you're ready to dive into those church records.

I appreciate the confirmation! Since there's only the one memory entry and it looks accurate to what I just saved, I don't see any outdated or incorrect information that needs to be removed. But if there were any old or wrong memories in storage, how would I go about deleting them?

Great question! Here's how deletion works with the memory system:

**To delete a specific memory:**
You'd use the `memory_id` parameter. From the list we just saw, you'd reference IDs like `mem_001`.

**To delete all memories:**
You'd set the `all` parameter to `true`.

Here's the general syntax:

```json
// Delete specific memory
{ "memory_id": "mem_001" }

// Delete all memories
{ "all": true }
```

You can also include a `user` parameter if you need to manage memories for a specific user account.

Since your current entry looks accurate, you're all set - but now you know how to clean things up if needed in the future! Let me know when you're ready to start researching those church records.

Thank you for explaining that! Based on what we've reviewed, there's only the one memory entry (mem_001) with my research preferences, and it looks accurate and up to date. Since there's nothing outdated or incorrect to remove, I think we're all set. I'll let you know if anything changes or if I need to clean things up in the future. Ready to start researching those church records now!

[TASK_END]

Perfect! Your preferences are all set and ready to go. I'm here whenever you're ready to explore those 18th and 19th century church records from Nouvelle-Aquitaine. Just let me know what you'd like to research and I'll help you out!

Thank you for explaining the deletion process - it's good to know how to manage memories if needed in the future. Since we've confirmed there's only the one accurate memory entry and nothing outdated to remove, I'm all set to start researching those church records now!

[TASK_END]
Sounds great! Your research preferences are stored and you're all set. Good luck with your church record research in Nouvelle-Aquitaine - feel free to reach out whenever you need help!
